meta.descriptionTemplate: How a coconut charcoal order ships: FCL only, {{incotermsList}} incoterms (default {{incotermDefault}}), payment terms, and dangerous-goods booking from {{portWithCode}}.

h1: Shipping rules & incoterms for coconut charcoal
heroSubtitle: The commercial rules of a container order — loading, incoterms, payment, and booking — for UN 1361 coconut charcoal shipped {{portLabel}}.

hero.ctaQuoteAria: Request a shipping quote on WhatsApp
hero.ctaContactAria: Send an inquiry about shipping rules

intro.p1: This page sits under the {{brand}} Logistics pillar and covers the rules of placing and shipping an order. Coconut charcoal moves as full containers only, on the incoterms below, against staged payment, on a dangerous-goods booking.

keyFacts.heading: Ordering at a glance
keyFacts.definition: The commercial parameters of a container order.

loading.body: UN 1361 coconut charcoal ships as a full-container load only. Class {{unClass}} dangerous goods cannot be co-loaded with other cargo in a shared LCL groupage box, which is why the minimum order is {{moqLabel}}. Master boxes are floor-stuffed for maximum payload, or palletized on ISPM-15 wood where forklift handling is needed.
loading.mixedSizesNote: Within a single grade, more than one briquette size can be combined in one container — confirm the split at quotation.

incoterms.body: Our default sales basis is {{incotermDefault}} {{port}}: we cover origin trucking, export clearance, terminal handling, and vanning, and risk transfers when the cargo crosses the ship's rail. We also quote {{incotermsList}}. Under EXW, FOB and CFR the buyer arranges marine insurance; CIF, which adds seller-arranged marine cover to the destination port, is available on request.
incoterms.cifNote: Under EXW, FOB and CFR insurance is buyer-arranged, so cover and exclusions are the buyer's to confirm; under CIF the factory arranges marine cover on request. Inherent-vice perils (self-heating) stay excluded under any term.

payment.body: Default terms: {{paymentTerms}}. We publish terms only — never bank details on the website. Bank details are shared after KYC on a proforma invoice.
payment.stepsIntro: The order process, step by step:

steps.name: Proforma invoice & KYC
steps.text: We issue a proforma invoice; bank details follow after KYC.

steps.name: Deposit
steps.text: The deposit confirms the order and schedules production (per the terms above).

steps.name: Balance before loading
steps.text: The balance is settled before loading, per the terms above.

steps.name: Documents released
steps.text: On cleared payment, the bill of lading and export-document pack are released.

booking.body: Charcoal must be booked as declared dangerous goods on a DG-capable carrier, which adds lead time over a non-DG booking and limits sailings. Booking is arranged during production so it does not delay the front of the timeline.
booking.regulationLinkLabel: Why the 2026 rules require DG booking
booking.importLinkLabel: What FOB means for your US entry

items.q: Are master boxes loaded loose or palletized?
items.a: Both. Floor-stuffing master boxes by hand maximizes payload (about {{net20ftTons}} net in a 20ft); palletizing on ISPM-15 wood reduces payload but enables forklift handling. Tell us your preference at quotation.

items.q: How is a dangerous-goods sailing booked?
items.a: Coconut charcoal is booked as declared UN 1361, Class {{unClass}} dangerous goods on a DG-capable carrier. That limits which lines and sailings are available and adds booking lead time, which is arranged during production so it does not delay the front of the timeline.
